sections.paragraph:
Ἰγνάτιος, ου, ὁ (on the name s. Hdb., Ergänzungsband 1923, 189. In addition to the examples given there of the name in this spelling see also Sb 8802b, 6 [82/83 a.d.]; 7662, 23 [II a.d.]) Ignatius, eccl. supervisor (bishop) of Antioch IEph IMg ITr IRo IPhld ISm IPol ins; Pol 9:1; 13:1f.

Hdb. Hdb. = Handbuch (in connection with a specific NT book, the ref. is to a commentary in the series Handbuch zum Neuen Testament, founded by HLietzmann)—List 6

Sb Sb = Sammelbuch griechischer Urkunden aus Aegypten—Lists 3, 4

eccl. eccl. = ecclesiastical

IEph IEph = Ignatius to the Ephesians—List 1

IMg IMg = Ignatius to the Magnesians—List 1

ITr ITr = Ignatius to the Trallians—List 1

IRo IRo = Ignatius to the Romans—List 1

IPhld IPhld = Ignatius to the Philadelphians—List 1

ISm ISm = Ignatius to the Smyrnaeans, I–II a.d.—List 1

IPol IPol = Ignatius to Polycarp—List 1

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

Pol Pol = Polycarp to the Philippians—List 1

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 466
